channel.title: yellow3 lab - Insights
channel.description: Perspectives on AI and what it makes possible. Writing from yellow3 lab on autonomous software, the future of digital products, and what European businesses need to understand about AI right now.

item.title: The Cost of Being Right
item.description: An AI law firm just won its first UK trial for about £400 in fees. The deeper story is the cost of enforcing a contract in Europe, and why LegalHQ starts before the dispute.
item.media:description: Magazine-style editorial image showing a legal desk with unpaid invoices, dispute papers, scales of justice, and a case summary showing £7,000 recovered for £400 in fees, illustrating why LegalHQ helps prevent contract disputes before they become costly.

item.title: The Abundance Problem
item.description: There has never been more choice in AI models, most of them open. The real risk has shifted to committing to the wrong one. What management should weigh.
item.media:description: A business leader stands at a crossroads between an overwhelming wall of AI model choices and a clearer strategic path, illustrating why management needs governance, flexibility, sovereignty, cost discipline, and continuous evaluation in an age of model abundance.

item.title: Who Holds the Off-Switch?
item.description: A US government letter switched off two frontier AI models for the entire planet in one afternoon. The real question for Europe was never whether government should have a say. It is who holds the switch, and what we do when it is not us.
item.media:description: A gloved hand flips a red industrial switch connected by glowing data cables to a digital map of Europe, symbolising Europe’s dependence on foreign-controlled AI infrastructure.

item.title: Five Times the Future Arrived
item.description: Mechanised weaving, electrification, personal computing, the internet, container shipping. Five technology transitions, one pattern. What history actually tells us about the question everyone is asking about AI and jobs.
item.media:description: Editorial illustration showing five stages of industrial transition arranged as a horizontal timeline: a mechanised loom from 1811, an electrified factory floor from 1900, a shipping container being loaded in 1956, a personal computer from 1987, and a network server rack from 1995.

item.title: The Gun Just Fired for European Founders. EU Inc. Is Here.
item.description: EU Inc. lets any European founder incorporate across 27 countries in 48 hours for under €100. The starting gun just fired. Here is what it means and what comes next.
item.media:description: A futuristic European founder pilots a sleek EU Inc. aircraft over Brussels as a glowing digital map of Europe and startup incorporation billboard symbolize the 28th regime, faster company formation, and a new runway for European entrepreneurs.

item.title: The Shopify Moment for Business Software Is Here
item.description: Before Shopify, building an online store required a developer and months of patience. The same shift is happening right now with business software - and the consequences for SMB growth are enormous.
item.media:description: A founder moves from a dark, cluttered workspace filled with disconnected spreadsheets, manual processes, old CRM tools, invoices, and workarounds into a bright modern office with a custom software dashboard showing growth, automation, sales, customers, inventory, and operations. The image visualizes how SMBs using custom-fit software can grow 2.8x faster by replacing fragmented generic tools with integrated systems built around their business.

item.title: I Analyzed What 50 AI Platforms Actually Cost to Build
item.description: A 788x price gap between AI models. $250/mo vs $2.3M/mo to build. A Copenhagen founder breaks down the real economics of AI platforms in 2026.

item.title: Europe's AI Slumber
item.description: The US controls 74% of global AI supercomputer capacity. Europe holds 4.8%. A European founder asks: are we regulating ourselves into irrelevance?

item.title: The Rise of Autonomous Software
item.description: Software that doesn't wait for instructions. The architecture, the implications, and the businesses it will create.

item.title: Your Software Should Work While You Sleep
item.description: The shift from tools that assist to software that acts. What autonomous systems mean for how businesses operate.
